name: Green Flash
primary_sources: Manchester Literary and Philosophical Society letters

distinctive_features: brief green spot above the Sun's upper limb
green ray shooting up from the horizon
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: appears as a small spot or ray, lasting 1-2 seconds
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: appears above the Sun's upper limb or as a ray from the horizon

domain_specific_visual.ATMOSPHERIC_LAYERING: enhanced by mirages and atmospheric refraction
domain_specific_visual.VISIBILITY_CONDITIONS: best seen in stable, clear air with an unobstructed horizon

functional_attributes.primary_function: optical phenomenon caused by atmospheric refraction

domain_specific_functional.OBSERVATION_CONTEXT: seen at sunrise or sunset, sometimes with the Moon or bright planets
domain_specific_functional.PHOTOGRAPHIC_RECORD: first color photograph taken at Vatican Observatory in 1960

relationships.description: The phenomenon is caused by the refraction of sunlight through the Earth's atmosphere.
Mirages increase the refraction, making the green flash more visible.

contextual_attributes.origin: global, observable from any location with a clear horizon
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: visible worldwide, more common over oceans and flat horizons
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: a rare and sought-after visual phenomenon for observers and photographers
primary_sources: Manchester Literary and Philosophical Society letters

contrastive_constraints: NOT a permanent or long-lasting phenomenon
NOT a cultural or mythological entity
